description.value:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.name: Purchased Traits
an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: As a maneuver, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 squares, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.
Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.
If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a maneuver to end one condition.
Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: Askeskogsmål

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

Skills: Survival, Perception, Stealth.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *First Blood*. Once per encounter, when you act on an ambush, pursuit, or hunt you identified first, gain an edge on the first related test.
Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

You do not temper the heat of battle within you. You unleash it! Your experience in the wild taught you the secrets of predators, and now, like the raptor, the panther, the wolf, you channel unfettered anger into martial prowess. Primordial Chaos is your ally. Let others use finesse to clean up the wreckage left in your wake. As a fury, you devastate foes with overwhelming might, hurl yourself and enemies around the battlefield, and grow stronger as your ferocity increases. Nature has no concept of fairness—and neither do you.

description.value:
Within the heat of battle, your determination and anger grow, fueling a Heroic Resource called ferocity.

Ferocity in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain ferocity equal to [[lookup @hero.victories]]{your Victories}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 ferocity.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you take damage, you [[/gain 1 hr]]{gain 1 ferocity}. The first time you become winded or are dying in an encounter, you [[/gain 1d3 hr]]{gain 1d3 ferocity}.

You lose any remaining ferocity at the end of the encounter.

Ferocity Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain ferocity outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost ferocity without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs ferocity, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited ferocity on its effect, such as To the Uttermost End, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of ferocity equal to your Victories.

Growing Ferocity

You gain certain benefits in combat based on the amount of ferocity you have (see 1st-Level Aspect Features for details). These benefits last until the end of your turn, even if a benefit would become unavailable to you because of the amount of ferocity you spend during your turn.

Some Growing Ferocity benefits can be applied only if you are a specific level or higher, with the level of those benefits noted in the various Growing Ferocity tables in this section.
You can’t obtain lower than a tier 2 outcome on any Might test made to jump.
You channel your ferocity into instinct and cunning, challenging the order of civilization. You have the Hide skill.

description.value:
You are never surprised. Additionally, whenever you would push a target with forced movement, you can slide them instead.

As your ferocity grows, you gain benefits as noted on the @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.classes.Item.0fkMLFZHJZKpAdM6]{Reaver Growing Ferocity} table. Benefits are cumulative except where an improved benefit replaces a lesser benefit.

You gain certain benefits in combat based on the amount of ferocity you have. These benefits last until the end of your turn, even if a benefit would become unavailable to you because of the amount of ferocity you spend during your turn. Some Growing Ferocity benefits can be applied only if you are a specific level or higher, with the level of those benefits noted in the various Growing Ferocity tables in this section.

Ferocity

Benefit

2

Whenever you use the Knockback maneuver, the forced movement distance gains a bonus equal to [[lookup @A]]{your Agility score}.

4

The first time you slide a creature on a turn, you [[/surge 1]].

6

You gain an edge on Agility tests and the Knockback maneuver

8 (4th Level)

The first time you slide a creature on a turn, you [[/surge 2]].

10 (7th Level)

You have a double edge on Agility tests and the Knockback maneuver.

12 (10th Level)

Whenever you use a heroic ability, you gain [[/heal 10 temporary]]. Additionally, whenever you make a power roll that imposes forced movement on a target, the forced movement distance gains a bonus equal to [[lookup @A]]{your Agility score}.

system.story: You are as elusive as a hummingbird.

effect.before: You take half the damage from the triggering effect and can shift up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup @A]]{your Agility score}.

spend.text: If the damage has any potency effects associated  with it, the potency is reduced by 1 for you.

description.value: If you want a good balance of protection, speed, and damage, the Panther kit is for you. This kit increases your Stamina not by wearing armor, but through the focused battle preparation of body and mind, letting you be fast and mobile while swinging a heavy weapon at your foes

system.story: The faster you move, the harder you hit

effect.after: You can move up to 3 squares straight toward the target before this strike, which deals extra damage equal to the number of squares you move this way

system.story: The heavy impact of your weapon attacks drives your foes ever back.
Teach them the folly of lining up for you.

effect.before: You move up to your speed in a straight line, and enemy squares are not difficult terrain for this movement. You can end this movement in a creature’s space and move them to an adjacent unoccupied space. You make one power roll that targets each enemy whose space you move through.
effect.after: The last target you damage takes [[/damage @M]]{extra damage} equal to your Might score for each opportunity attack you trigger during your move.

system.story: See how well they fight after you’ve bled them dry.

effect.after: You can deal [[/damage 1d6]] damage to yourself to deal an extra [[/damage 1d6]] damage to the target.

effect.before: Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying can’t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature can’t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they can’t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creature’s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mount’s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the rider’s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value:
Solveig the Burner is a former woodcutter turned charcoal-burner who has lived alone in the Ashen Woods (the remains of the Greenweald) for three years. She is thin—terribly thin—with skin stained grey-black by years of charcoal dust. Her clothes are ash-coloured furs. She carries a handaxe and a leather satchel.

Solveig refused to leave when the Greenweald was put to the torch. She burns charcoal for fuel, navigates by root-patterns beneath the ash, and avoids blighted beasts. She hasn't spoken to another person in four months.

She is a stubborn, practical survivor—not a quest-giver, not a tragic victim, but a human who decided the Ashen Woods are her home now.
biography.director:
Solveig appears in Beat 6, Part 6 (The Charcoal Cathedral)—a Point of Interest between Montage Rounds 2 and 3.

She shows herself only if the PCs leave an offering at the 'cathedral' altar. Once she trusts them:

  • 'You're heading for the green place.' (Not a question.) She knows about the Green Heart—seen 'a wall of thorns and light' from a distance.
  • Root-system lore: 'The ground is alive under the dead. The roots glow at night—amber, like your friend's eye.' (Connecting to Lew's amber eye.)
  • Rootgnaw warning: 'There are things that chew the green roots. Big as dogs. Bigger.'
  • If treated well: shares a shortcut through the cathedral (+1 edge on one test in Round 3).

She wants news and a knife (hers is worn to a nub). She will talk for hours if allowed.

If harmed or robbed: vanishes into the ash. She knows these woods better than anyone alive. They will not find her.

In Beat 8 (return journey), the offering spot is empty. A charcoal stick broken in half is left—'Either she's moved on, or she's telling us to.'
